message:
perf(runtime): lock-free timed_count, SmallVec steal, and 3-phase next_task

global_injector.rs: Add timed_count atomic alongside pending_count
and ready_count to enable lock-free emptiness checks for the timed
lane.  pop_timed(), peek_earliest_deadline(), pop_timed_if_due(),
is_empty(), and has_timed_work() now return early when timed_count
is zero, avoiding the timed_queue mutex acquisition entirely on
the common path.  inject_timed() increments counters before push
so timed_count >= heap.len() always holds.  Also replaces the
fetch_update CAS loop in saturating_decrement with a simpler
fetch_sub + conditional store(0), since brief underflow is harmless
for these advisory Relaxed counters.

local_queue.rs: Replace Vec<TaskId> with SmallVec<[TaskId; 8]> for
skipped_locals in steal() and steal_batch(), keeping the common
case of few local tasks allocation-free.  Extract steal_batch_locked
helper and acquire the source/dest locks in deterministic pointer
order to prevent ABBA deadlock when two workers steal from each
other concurrently.  Adds tests: steal_batch_many_skipped_locals
(16 local tasks + 1 remote) and concurrent_bidirectional_steal_batch
(2 threads, 64 tasks, verifies no deadlock and no task loss).

three_lane.rs: Rewrite next_task() from sequential try_cancel/
try_timed/try_ready calls (each acquiring the local PriorityScheduler
lock independently) into a 5-phase pipeline:
  1. Global queues (lock-free / own mutex)
  2. Fast ready paths (local_ready, fast_queue, global ready)
  3. Single local PriorityScheduler lock for cancel+timed+ready
  4. Steal from other workers
  5. Fallback cancel (streak-limit exceeded path)
Phases 1-2 cover the hot path; Phase 3 replaces 3 lock acquisitions
with 1 for the local scheduler fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
